title: ABNF, Tokens, Whitespace, Line Endings, Limits, and Parser Architecture · Language Lens: Token Control Validation
summary: A parser splits strings freely, accepts bare line feeds and whitespace before fields, has no byte limits, and mixes syntax recognition with application side effects. Planned artifact: Add and verify the token control validation increment in strict bounded incremental parser architecture. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate tokens and visible bytes using octet classes while rejecting separators, controls, NUL, and forbidden whitespace.
Translate RFC ABNF alternatives, repetition, terminals, lists, and prose constraints into explicit parser states and tests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Token Control Validation · Theory Token Control Validation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 1769 in a privacy-sensitive proxy translating between two parser implementations. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate strict grammar and resource limits before any application effect; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then block extra QPACK streams and fall back without removing HTTP/2 availability. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate tokens and visible bytes using octet classes while rejecting separators, controls, NUL, and forbidden whitespace.
